name: faq_shipping_returns_8_pages_help-center

observed_strengths.observation: Functional Help Center at /pages/help-center — page loads and is well-structured with three clear H2 sections: 'Drinking Mud' (product experience), 'Accounts & Subscriptions' (retention), 'Orders & Shipping' (post-purchase)

'Drinking Mud' FAQ section with H3 questions about caffeine, ingredients, sourcing, organic status, and pregnancy safety — objection handling for health-conscious buyers embedded in a discoverable help page
'Accounts & Subscriptions' H2 section — subscription management FAQ is prominently included in the help center, supporting subscriber retention by making subscription changes easy to find

Meta description is specific to product experience questions ('What does mud taste like?', 'Does it taste like coffee?', 'What is sediment?') — this help center targets first-time buyer uncertainty directly

missing_or_weak.observation: No email capture heading visible on Help Center page — unlike deathwishcoffee which included email capture on the FAQ page, mudwtr's help center doesn't appear to include an email capture section in the visible heading structure

No product purchase CTA in FAQ headings — visitors who arrive via 'does MUD\WTR have caffeine?' search intent are answered but not immediately funneled to a PDP or subscription offer after their question is resolved

purchase_path_notes: Help center is well-organized and functional. The primary conversion opportunity is adding product CTAs at the bottom of FAQ answers to capture visitors who arrive via ingredient-intent search queries.
